questions.question: Why does a Vision Transformer use patches instead of feeding one pixel per token?
options: Pixel-per-token sequences explode attention compute quadratically and lose tractability at typical resolutions
questions.explanation: A 224x224 image is 150528 pixel tokens. Attention is quadratic in length, so 16x16 patching shrinks the sequence to 196 tokens and makes the model tractable.

questions.question: Why is the 2D sinusoidal position embedding deterministic instead of learned?
options: Fixed sin/cos signals interpolate cleanly to grids the model never saw, which is useful when resolution changes
questions.explanation: Fixed sinusoidal positions are deterministic across resolutions and let the same encoder run on inputs it was not trained on.

questions.question: What is the output sequence length of a 224x224 image with patch_size=16 and one CLS token?
questions.explanation: 224/16 = 14, so 14x14 = 196 patches, plus one CLS token for 197 total tokens.

questions.question: Why is reconstruction (unfold then unflatten) a useful sanity check on the patch front end?
options: Round-tripping pixels through patch-flatten with no projection must equal the input; failure means the unfold math is wrong
questions.explanation: The patch step is invertible without projection. If the round-trip drifts, the unfold logic is bugged and the rest of the encoder is unsafe.

questions.question: What changes when you swap a learned position embedding for the 2D sinusoidal one in this lesson?
options: Learned positions can fit a fixed resolution slightly better; sinusoidal positions transfer to new resolutions without retraining
Sinusoidal positions are random per step
questions.explanation: Learned positions are flexible at one resolution. Sinusoidal positions cost zero parameters and survive resolution shifts.

questions.question: What is the relationship between Conv2d with kernel = stride = patch_size and flatten-then-linear?
options: Conv2d is a faster way to spell the same linear projection over each patch
questions.explanation: Each Conv2d output location dot-products the patch pixels with one filter, which is identical to flatten-then-linear.
